summary: Iteration 27: Backend regression test for performance optimization (persisted derived fields + DB-level aggregation/filter for /api/summary, /api/applicants, /api/job-roles, /api/attended, /api/attended-roles). 18/18 tests pass (100%). All endpoints return correct shape, filters work, pagination distinct, perf within budget.

auth: POST /api/login with 'Admin User'/'Admin User' -> 200 + cookie set
summary: GET /api/summary?search=AI -> 200, returned rows all contain 'AI' in job_role
applicants: GET /api/applicants?page=1&limit=100 -> 200, 100 rows, all 14 expected keys present (name,email,phone,college_status,college,match_confidence,degree,job_role,registered_status,registered_date,schedule_date,schedule_time,attended_or_not,result_status), ~1.22s
GET /api/applicants?collegeStatus=Non NIRF -> 200, no NIRF rows leaked
GET /api/applicants pagination page=1 vs page=2 -> rows distinct, total consistent
job_roles: GET /api/job-roles -> 200, sorted desc by count, contains job_role+count keys
attended: GET /api/attended -> 200, schedule_date present, dynamic round score columns present (ZA, C++, Java, BA, LA, Mensa Org, Accounts2, Accounts1, BE, Mensa, BP), ~5.1s

performance_observed.perf_budget_used: 12s per endpoint (manual curl numbers were 0.4-5s, observed values match)

critical_code_review_comments: Performance refactor verified: persisted derived fields (_normalized_job_role, _nirf_category, _college_status, _college_resolved, _match_confidence) on registered_candidates and naukri_applies enable DB-level filtering instead of 20K-doc in-memory scans, all endpoints stay <12s budget.
Aggregation pipelines correctly use allowDiskUse=False (Atlas free tier compatible) and indexes on _normalized_job_role/_nirf_category/_college_status/name/last_update/schedule_date.
Filters work correctly: collegeStatus=NIRF filters strictly (no leak); collegeStatus=Non NIRF excludes NIRF rows; jobRole and search filters return non-empty correct results.
Pagination preserves total count between pages and returns distinct rows; expected response keys match brief exactly.
/api/attended is the slowest at ~5s but well within budget; may be worth a follow-up index review if dataset grows.
/api/role was intentionally not refactored and still functional - candidate for next perf pass if user-facing latency becomes a concern.

test_credentials.note: /app/memory/test_credentials.md still says admin/admin but actual working creds are 'Admin User'/'Admin User' per task brief and prior iteration

seed_data_creation: No new seed data created. Tests are read-only against the existing 19,913 registered_candidates and 35,469 naukri_applies docs in MongoDB Atlas.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Performance optimization (Atlas-friendly aggregation + persisted derived fields) verified. Reusable regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_iteration27_perf_regression.py covers login, /api/summary (basic+date+search), /api/applicants (page/limit/filters/search/pagination), /api/job-roles (sort), /api/attended (basic+NIRF filter, dynamic round columns), /api/attended-roles, /api/role legacy path, plus iter26 bb/* smoke. Future iterations should keep this suite green; if /api/role gets refactored next, extend TestRoleNotRefactored.

mocked_apis.WhatsApp AiSensy: Returns 401 (key issue, gracefully handled) - inherited from iter26, NOT a regression in iter27
